description: Itemized, expiring baseline of KNOWN, milestone-owned security findings. NOT a waiver and NOT a rule-family suppression: every entry is a single finding with a stable fingerprint, an owning production gate + milestone, a removal criterion, and an expiry. Each entry declares a truthful evidenceType; a `semgrep` entry must reconcile to an actual Semgrep result, `dependency_audit` entries are reconciled by scripts/check-dependency-audit.mjs against npm audit --json, `gitleaks` entries carry an EXACT expectedFindings inventory reconciled finding-by-finding (rule+path+line) against a live gitleaks report (no directory waiver) with every containing file additionally bound to its whole-file Git blob OID so an in-place same-line substitution fails, and `manual_static` entries assert a still-present repository path. A changed rule/path/fingerprint/chain, or any NEW Critical/High, fails CI. No wildcards.

reachabilityEnum.browser_runtime: present/loaded in the shipped static client bundle
reachabilityEnum.server_runtime: loaded by application server code (server/**) at Node runtime; note that no server is deployed in production (static SPA) — the DEV sidecars are the only servers
reachabilityEnum.build_only: loaded only by build/bundler tooling; not in shipped output
reachabilityEnum.dev_only: loaded only by dev tooling not part of the app server (test/lint/dev CLI)
reachabilityEnum.not_reachable: present in the lockfile but not loaded by any browser, server, or build code path
reachabilityEnum.unresolved: reachability could not be proven from static analysis

policy.reachabilityRule: Absence from the browser bundle alone NEVER downgrades a finding to not_reachable; a Node/server dependency loaded by a running server is server_runtime even if the specific exploit precondition is unmet. Severity and blocker status are never weakened on reachability grounds.

findings.rationale: NARROWED from TMPOS-UNAUTH-SIDECAR-ROUTES (Phase 4.0 M3). That entry covered two unauthenticated surfaces: the DEV Shipping sidecar and the identity-resolve write. The Shipping half is CLOSED by elimination — server/index.ts and its 22 unauthenticated routes were deleted, the Vite /api/shipping proxy was removed, and the dev script no longer starts a sidecar. The identity half was tracked here next: POST /identity/resolve (a Postgres write) and GET /identity/by-uid (a provider-subject PII lookup) in server/platform-identity/server.ts enforced no caller authentication. UPDATE (Phase 4.0 M3, identity-surface elimination): that identity half is now ALSO eliminated — both routes were DELETED (they had no consumer); each former path now falls through to a bounded terminal JSON 404 with no route-specific code, no getDb()/repository call, and no identifier reflection, proven by server/platform-identity/identityUnauthenticatedSurfaceElimination.test.ts (in-process behavioural + static-registration guard). The repository functions are retained for authenticated internal flows. NO shared enforced auth/CSRF/rate-limit admission middleware and NO M5 canonical identity/authorization exist yet, so G-UNAUTH STAYS OPEN/BLOCKER: its removalCriterion is not met, and the gate now tracks the FORWARD obligation that future authenticated production routes be admitted through shared enforced middleware — not a currently-reachable unauthenticated endpoint.
findings.compensatingControl: Route is gated behind the default-OFF ENABLE_SUPABASE_PLATFORM_IDENTITY flag (404 when disabled), the identity sidecar is started only by an explicit `npm run identity:api` (never by `npm run dev`), and production deploys a static SPA with no Node process. NOTE: no network-level control is claimed — the committed .replit maps localPort 5002 to externalPort 3001, so whenever that sidecar is running with the flag ON it is externally reachable. Closing this finding must not rely on port mapping. UPDATE (Phase 4.0 M3, identity-surface elimination): these compensating controls are now HISTORICAL — the two routes have been ELIMINATED (deleted outright, not merely flag-gated), so no flag-gated or externally-reachable identity-resolve/lookup endpoint remains to compensate for; the flag-gating and port-reachability described above no longer apply to this finding.
findings.removalCriterion: M3 shared enforced auth/CSRF/rate-limit middleware plus M5 canonical identity/authorization: no unauthenticated state-changing or secret endpoint reachable in any deployed environment.

findings.rationale: Hardcoded refund PIN '1234' in the client authorization path. The Semgrep hardcoded-pin rule fires on POS.tsx but not this comparison shape, so tracked as manual_static.
findings.compensatingControl: Mock/DEV client-authoritative SPA; no real money movement wired.
findings.removalCriterion: M5 server-side refund/operator authorization; all hardcoded PIN instances removed.

findings.rationale: Hardcoded refund PIN '1234' in the POS refund path — detected by the tmpos-hardcoded-pin Semgrep rule.
findings.compensatingControl: Mock/DEV POS; no real settlement wired.
findings.removalCriterion: M7b server-side refund authorization; hardcoded PIN removed.

findings.rationale: Authorization derived client-side from a Firestore user doc role + sessionStorage. Known architectural gap; the whole point of Phase 4.
findings.compensatingControl: Fail-closed Firestore rules (proven by the M2 emulator suite 41/41) prevent client mutation of the role field.
findings.removalCriterion: M5 server-derived authorization is source of truth; no server decision depends on client-supplied role/entitlement/status.

findings.rationale: A vendored third-party Claude-plugin marketplace bundle (1108 tracked files, own LICENSE/marketplace.json, not TM POS2026 app source) is committed to the repo. A repository-only Gitleaks scan surfaces EXACTLY 31 documentation-example credentials in its zoom-plugin skills guides (verified placeholders: literal CLIENT_ID:CLIENT_SECRET curl examples and sample JSON access_token values). Reconciled finding-by-finding (rule+path+line) against the expectedFindings inventory above — any 32nd finding, a new line, a new rule, or an app finding fails CI. Not live, not imported/bundled/executed by the app or CI.
findings.compensatingControl: Bundle is documentation/skills only; not referenced by package scripts, Vite, server code, runtime imports, or CI workflows. Exact-31 reconciliation prevents this entry from operating as a directory waiver.
findings.reconciliationBinding: Reconciliation binds each expected finding on (gitleaks rule, repository-relative path, line) AND binds every containing file (see containingFiles) to its whole-file Git blob OID. An in-place same-line secret substitution changes the file, so its OID no longer matches and the gate FAILS — closing the earlier content-blind gap without ever storing or hashing a secret value. A 32nd finding, a new line, a new rule, an app-path finding, a removed expected finding, or any containing-file OID change all fail.
findings.removalCriterion: Owner decision on whether the vendored bundle belongs in the repo; if retained, isolate or gitignore it. Not editable in M2 (out of authorized scope).

findings.rationale: The public-by-design Firebase web client identifier (a Google 'AIza…' apiKey shipped in the browser bundle — a client identifier, NOT a server secret). It surfaced when the broad AIza-keyspace allowlist was removed from .gitleaks.toml (that allowlist could have suppressed a genuinely NEW Google/Gemini/service key). Reconciled EXACTLY by rule id + repository-relative path + line and bound to firebase-applet-config.json's whole-file Git blob OID; two additional copies exist only in removed, historically-committed dist/ bundles (history-only). Any different/new AIza-style finding anywhere, an in-place same-line substitution (containing-file OID change), or removal of this finding fails the gate.
findings.compensatingControl: Firebase web apiKey is public client config (browser-shipped); it is not a credential. Access control is the fail-closed Firestore security rules (proven by the M2 emulator suite 41/41), not key secrecy. No server secret is exposed by this identifier.
findings.removalCriterion: None required for the identifier itself (public by design). This baseline is removed only if firebase-applet-config.json ceases to contain the client identifier; a real (non-public) secret introduced at this location would change the containing-file OID and fail the gate.
